title: Running 128 Coding Agents at Once
summary: Cursor's Sam Whitmore discusses scaling agent deployment with Baseten's Charlie O'Neill and Harry Partridge, covering the transition from managing parallel tasks to building agent communication systems. The focus is on running hundreds of coding agents simultaneously with inter-agent messaging and review capabilities.
writeup: Cursor's Sam Whitmore talks with Baseten's Charlie O'Neill and Harry Partridge about scaling coding agents from a handful of parallel tasks to hundreds running at once. The conversation centers on the shift from managing parallel tasks toward building systems where agents communicate with each other, including inter-agent messaging and review capabilities. The framing matters because orchestrating 128 agents simultaneously raises coordination problems that single-agent or small-batch setups do not face.
